question:
Hot water at 66.0°C / 4.6 MPa (2.8 kg/s) heats liquid R-134a at 17.0°C / 1.48 MPa (8.1 kg/s) in a heat exchanger. The hot water exits at 45.0°C. Both streams remain liquid. T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine Q̇, T_c_out, Ṡ_gen, Ẋ_dest, and η_II.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg
h_c_in = ___ kJ/kg
Q̇ = ___ kW
h_c_out = ___ kJ/kg
T_c_out = ___ °C
s_h_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_h_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Assume an adiabatic heat exchanger to the surroundings, negligible pressure drops, and both streams remain liquid.

For the hot water, use the compressed-liquid approximation:
- \(h(T,P)\approx h_f(T)+v_f(T)\,[P-P_{sat}(T)]\)
- \(s(T,P)\approx s_f(T)\)

For the liquid R-134a, use the incompressible-liquid approximation with \(c_{p,\ell}\approx 1.42\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\).  
For the listed absolute \(h\) and \(s\) values, I used the common refrigeration-table reference \(h=s=0\) at saturated liquid \(-40^\circ\text{C}\). With any other reference, only the absolute \(h\) and \(s\) values shift; \( \dot Q,\ T_{c,out},\ \dot S_{gen},\ \dot X_{dest},\ \eta_{II}\) are essentially unchanged.

1) Heat transfer rate from hot water
\[
\dot Q=\dot m_h(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})
\]
Using water properties at 66°C and 45°C:
\[
h_{h,in}\approx 280.90,\quad h_{h,out}\approx 193.05\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\dot Q=2.8(280.90-193.05)=245.98\ \text{kW}
\]

2) Cold-stream outlet enthalpy and temperature
\[
h_{c,out}=h_{c,in}+\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}
\]
\[
\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}=\frac{245.98}{8.1}=30.37\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
With \(h_{c,in}\approx 80.94\ \text{kJ/kg}\),
\[
h_{c,out}\approx 111.31\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
and
\[
T_{c,out}=17.0+\frac{30.37}{1.42}=38.40^\circ\text{C}
\]

3) Entropy generation
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_h(s_{h,out}-s_{h,in})+\dot m_c(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})
\]
Using:
\[
s_{h,in}\approx 0.9054,\quad s_{h,out}\approx 0.6377
\]
\[
s_{c,in}\approx 0.3103,\quad s_{c,out}\approx 0.4114
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}=2.8(0.6377-0.9054)+8.1(0.4114-0.3103)
=0.0693\ \text{kW/K}
\]

4) Exergy destruction
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}
\]
with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\):
\[
\dot X_{dest}=298.15(0.0693)=20.66\ \text{kW}
\]

5) Second-law efficiency
For a heat exchanger,
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\text{exergy gain of cold stream}}{\text{exergy loss of hot stream}}
=1-\frac{\dot X_{dest}}{\dot Ex_{hot,loss}}
\]
\[
\dot Ex_{hot,loss}
=\dot m_h\Big[(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})-T_0(s_{h,in}-s_{h,out})\Big]
\approx 22.51\ \text{kW}
\]
\[
\dot Ex_{cold,gain}
=\dot m_c\Big[(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})-T_0(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})\Big]
\approx 1.85\ \text{kW}
\]
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{1.85}{22.51}=0.0818
\]

h_h_out = 193.05 kJ/kg
h_c_in = 80.94 kJ/kg
Q̇ = 245.98 kW
h_c_out = 111.31 kJ/kg
T_c_out = 38.40 °C
s_h_in = 0.9054 kJ/(kg·K)
s_h_out = 0.6377 kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_in = 0.3103 kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = 0.4114 kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = 0.0693 kW/K
Ẋ_dest = 20.66 kW
η_II = 0.0818

question:
Hot water at 82.0°C / 1.6 MPa (1.8 kg/s) heats liquid R-134a at 13.0°C / 1.45 MPa (8.0 kg/s) in a heat exchanger. The hot water exits at 39.0°C. Both streams remain liquid. T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine Q̇, T_c_out, Ṡ_gen, Ẋ_dest, and η_II.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg
h_c_in = ___ kJ/kg
Q̇ = ___ kW
h_c_out = ___ kJ/kg
T_c_out = ___ °C
s_h_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_h_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using a steady, adiabatic heat-exchanger model with negligible KE/PE changes and treating both streams as compressed liquids:

- Energy balance:
\[
\dot Q = \dot m_h\,(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})=\dot m_c\,(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})
\]

- For liquid streams, use the compressed-liquid/incompressible approximation:
\[
\Delta h \approx c_p \Delta T,\qquad \Delta s \approx c_p \ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)
\]

I used:
- water: \(c_{p,w}\approx 4.18\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- liquid R-134a near 13–42°C: \(c_{p,R}\approx 1.40\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

So,

\[
\dot Q = 1.8(4.18)(82-39)=323.5\ \text{kW}
\]

For the R-134a,
\[
\Delta h_c=\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}=\frac{323.5}{8.0}=40.44\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

and
\[
\Delta T_c \approx \frac{40.44}{1.40}=28.9^\circ\text{C}
\]
\[
T_{c,out}\approx 13.0+28.9=41.9^\circ\text{C}
\]

Entropy generation for the adiabatic exchanger:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_h(s_{h,out}-s_{h,in})+\dot m_c(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}\approx 1.8(-0.5395)+8(0.1346)=0.106\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}=298.15(0.106)\approx 31.6\ \text{kW}
\]

Second-law efficiency for the exchanger:
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\text{exergy gained by cold stream}}{\text{exergy lost by hot stream}}
\]
\[
\eta_{II}
=\frac{\dot m_c[(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})-T_0(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})]}
{\dot m_h[(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})-T_0(s_{h,in}-s_{h,out})]}
\approx 0.073
\]

Note: the absolute \(h\) and \(s\) values of liquid R-134a depend on the property-table reference state. The process results \(\dot Q\), \(T_{c,out}\), \(\dot S_{gen}\), \(\dot X_{dest}\), and \(\eta_{II}\) do not.

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 46.0°C and 5.4 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 313.0°C (constant pressure). Determine the heat input per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For a boiler operating at steady state with negligible kinetic/potential energy changes and no shaft work,

\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]

### 1) Inlet state: compressed liquid, 46.0°C and 5.4 MPa
For compressed liquid water, use the common approximation

\[
h_{in}\approx h_f(T)+v_f(T)\,[P-P_{sat}(T)]
\]

At \(T=46.0^\circ C\), saturated-liquid properties are approximately:

- \(h_f \approx 192.6\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

question:

\]

- Entropy balance per unit mass:
\[
s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]

- **Inlet water, 42.0°C**: treated as a compressed liquid with the usual approximation  
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(42^\circ\mathrm{C}),\qquad s_{in}\approx s_f(42^\circ\mathrm{C})
  \]

\[

\]
\[
s_{gen}=7.1546-0.5989-\frac{3222.7}{1555.0}=4.4832\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

s_gen = 4.4832 kJ/(kg·K)

For entropy generation per unit mass in the boiler:

\[
s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]

with \(T_{source} = 1857.0\ \text{K}\).

### 1) Inlet state: compressed liquid water at 45.0°C, 1.9 MPa
Use the compressed-liquid approximation from saturated liquid properties at 45°C:

\[
h_{in} \approx h_f(45^\circ C) + v_f(45^\circ C)\,(P-P_{sat})
\]

Using \(h_f \approx 188.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(v_f \approx 0.00101\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\), and \(P_{sat}(45^\circ C)\approx 9.6\ \text{kPa}\),

\[
h_{in} \approx 188.4 + 0.00101(1900-9.6) \approx 190.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
s_{in} \approx s_f(45^\circ C) \approx 0.6380\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

= 3168.1\ \text{kJ/kg}

\]

\[
s_{gen} = 6.6591 - 1.7060 = 4.9531\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

\[

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 76.0°C and 7.7 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 488.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1220.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg

s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady-flow boiler relations per unit mass, neglecting KE/PE and shaft work:

- Energy balance:
  \[
  q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
  \]

- Entropy balance with heat supplied from a source at \(T_H=1220\ \text{K}\):
  \[
  s_{gen}=(s_{out}-s_{in})-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
  \]

- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{dest}=T_0\,s_{gen}
  \]

- Second-law efficiency of the boiler:
  \[
  \eta_{II}=
  \frac{(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}
       {\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right)q_{in}}
  \]

Property evaluation:

- Inlet compressed liquid at \(76.0^\circ\text{C},\,7.7\ \text{MPa}\):
  use compressed-liquid approximation
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(76^\circ\text{C})+v_f(76^\circ\text{C})\,[P-P_{sat}(76^\circ\text{C})]
  \]
  and \(s_{in}\approx s_f(76^\circ\text{C})\).

- Outlet at \(7.7\ \text{MPa},\,488.0^\circ\text{C}\):
  read from superheated steam tables.

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 50.0°C and 4.0 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 304.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1013.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady-flow boiler relations per unit mass, with negligible kinetic/potential energy changes and no shaft work:

1. **Inlet state**: compressed liquid at \(50.0^\circ\text{C}, 4.0\ \text{MPa}\)

Approximate compressed-liquid properties from saturated liquid at \(50^\circ\text{C}\):

\[
h_{in}\approx h_f(50^\circ\text{C})+v_f(50^\circ\text{C})\,[P-P_{sat}(50^\circ\text{C})]
\]

Using steam-table values near \(50^\circ\text{C}\):
\[
h_f \approx 209.3,\quad v_f \approx 0.001012\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg},\quad s_f \approx 0.7038
\]

\[
h_{in}\approx 209.3+0.001012(4000-12.35)\approx 213.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

For entropy, the pressure effect on a compressed liquid is very small, so:
\[
s_{in}\approx s_f(50^\circ\text{C})\approx 0.7038\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

2. **Outlet state**: superheated steam at \(4.0\ \text{MPa}, 304.0^\circ\text{C}\)

From superheated steam tables (interpolated at \(4.0\ \text{MPa}, 304^\circ\text{C}\)):

\[
h_{out}\approx 2970.6\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_{out}\approx 6.379\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

3. **Heat input**
\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]
\[
q_{in}=2970.6-213.4=2757.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4. **Entropy generation**

Steady-state entropy balance:
\[
s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
\]
with \(T_H=1013.0\ \text{K}\):

\[
s_{gen}=6.379-0.7038-\frac{2757.2}{1013.0}
\]
\[
s_{gen}\approx 2.954\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

5. **Exergy destruction**
\[
x_{dest}=T_0\,s_{gen}
\]
with \(T_0=298.15\ \text{K}\):

\[
x_{dest}=298.15(2.954)\approx 880.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

6. **Second-law efficiency**

Exergy transferred with heat:
\[
x_Q=q_{in}\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right)
\]

Increase in flow exergy of the water:
\[
\Delta \psi=(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})
\]

Thus
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta \psi}{x_Q}
=1-\frac{x_{dest}}{x_Q}
\]

Numerically:
\[
x_Q=2757.2\left(1-\frac{298.15}{1013.0}\right)\approx 1945.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\Delta\psi=2757.2-298.15(6.379-0.7038)\approx 1065.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{1065.1}{1945.7}\approx 0.548
\]

Final answers:

h_in = 213.4 kJ/kg  
h_out = 2970.6 kJ/kg  
q_in = 2757.2 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.379 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 2.954 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 880.6 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.548

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa. Stream 1: 179.0°C, 1.1 kg/s. Stream 2: 48.0°C, 7.9 kg/s. Find the exit temperature.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
raw_response:
Since \(T_{\text{sat}}(1.2\ \text{MPa}) \approx 188^\circ\text{C}\), both inlet streams are compressed liquids.

For liquid water, use the compressed-liquid approximation:
\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)+v_f(T)\,[p-p_{\text{sat}}(T)]
\]

Then apply steady adiabatic mixing:

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.1+7.9=9.0\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using water-property values for the two inlet states gives approximately:
- \(h_1 \approx 759.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(h_2 \approx 202.2\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

So,
\[
h_3=\frac{(1.1)(759.0)+(7.9)(202.2)}{9.0}\approx 270.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), this enthalpy corresponds to a compressed-liquid exit temperature of about \(64.3^\circ\text{C}\).

h_1 = 759.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 202.2 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 9.0 kg/s  
h_3 = 270.2 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 64.3 °C
response_text:
Since \(T_{\text{sat}}(1.2\ \text{MPa}) \approx 188^\circ\text{C}\), both inlet streams are compressed liquids.

For liquid water, use the compressed-liquid approximation:
\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)+v_f(T)\,[p-p_{\text{sat}}(T)]
\]

Then apply steady adiabatic mixing:

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.1+7.9=9.0\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using water-property values for the two inlet states gives approximately:
- \(h_1 \approx 759.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(h_2 \approx 202.2\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

So,
\[
h_3=\frac{(1.1)(759.0)+(7.9)(202.2)}{9.0}\approx 270.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), this enthalpy corresponds to a compressed-liquid exit temperature of about \(64.3^\circ\text{C}\).

h_1 = 759.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 202.2 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 9.0 kg/s  
h_3 = 270.2 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 64.3 °C

\[
h(T,p) \approx h_f(T)
\]

So from the saturated liquid water table:

- At \(181.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx h_f(181^\circ\text{C})\)
- At \(50.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx h_f(50^\circ\text{C})\)

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=8.5+8.9=17.4\ \text{kg/s}
\]

\[

\]

\[

\]

Then using compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid data again, \(h_3=481.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to approximately:

\[
T_3 \approx 114.8^\circ\text{C}
\]

h_2 = ___ kJ/kg

Both streams are **compressed liquid water** at 2.0 MPa since \(T \ll T_{\text{sat}}(2.0\text{ MPa})\approx 212^\circ\text{C}\).  
For compressed liquid water, use the common approximation:

\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)
\]

From saturated-liquid water data:

- at \(106.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 444.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- at \(50.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 209.3\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Adiabatic mixing gives:

\[

\]

\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(7.6)(444.4)+(3.5)(209.3)}{11.1}\approx 370.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At 2.0 MPa, \(h_3\approx 370.3\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to a compressed-liquid temperature of about \(88.3^\circ\text{C}\).

question:
Water at 179.0°C (1.9 kg/s) mixes adiabatically with water at 45.0°C (5.8 kg/s) at a constant pressure of 0.9 MPa. Find the exit temperature and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
For a steady, adiabatic mixer at constant pressure:

- Mass balance:
  \[
  \dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.9+5.8=7.7\ \text{kg/s}
  \]

- At \(P=0.9\ \text{MPa}\), the saturation temperature is about \(175.4^\circ\text{C}\), so stream 1 at \(179.0^\circ\text{C}\) is slightly superheated vapor.

Using steam-table values:

- Stream 1 \((0.9\ \text{MPa}, 179.0^\circ\text{C})\):
  \[
  h_1 \approx 2780.1\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_1 \approx 6.641\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

- Stream 2 is compressed liquid at \(45.0^\circ\text{C}\), approximated as saturated liquid at the same temperature:
  \[
  h_2 \approx 188.4\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_2 \approx 0.639\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

Energy balance for the adiabatic mixer:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]
\[
h_3=\frac{(1.9)(2780.1)+(5.8)(188.4)}{7.7}\approx 827.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(0.9\ \text{MPa}\), this lies between \(h_f\) and \(h_g\), so the exit is a saturated mixture. Hence:
\[
T_3 = T_{\text{sat}}(0.9\ \text{MPa}) \approx 175.4^\circ\text{C}
\]

From saturated-mixture relations at \(0.9\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
s_3 \approx 2.290\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

Entropy balance for a steady adiabatic mixer:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}=(7.7)(2.290)-(1.9)(6.641)-(5.8)(0.639)\approx 1.31\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_1 = 2780.1 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 188.4 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 7.7 kg/s  
h_3 = 827.9 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 175.4 °C  
s_1 = 6.641 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.639 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 2.290 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 1.31 kW/K

For compressed liquid water, use the common approximation:

\[
h(T,P)\approx h_f(T), \qquad s(T,P)\approx s_f(T)
\]

From saturated-liquid water properties at the given temperatures:

- At \(157.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 662.5\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 1.913\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- At \(54.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 226.1\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2 \approx 0.755\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

\]

Entropy rate balance for adiabatic steady mixing:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=(12.5)(1.451)-(7.1)(1.913)-(5.4)(0.755)=0.481\ \text{kW/K}

question:
Water at 146.0°C (3.0 kg/s) mixes adiabatically with water at 37.0°C (3.0 kg/s) at a constant pressure of 1.5 MPa. Find the exit temperature and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
At \(P=1.5\ \text{MPa}\), the saturation temperature of water is about \(198^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams are compressed liquids.  
Using the compressed-liquid approximation,

\[
h(T,P)\approx h_f(T), \qquad s(T,P)\approx s_f(T)
\]

and applying steady, adiabatic mixing:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

with

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=3.0+3.0=6.0\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Since the two inlet mass flow rates are equal,

\[
h_3=\frac{h_1+h_2}{2}
\]

Using compressed-liquid (saturated-liquid) water properties at the given temperatures:

- At \(146^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 614.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 1.801\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- At \(37^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 154.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2 \approx 0.531\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then

\[
h_3=\frac{614.9+154.9}{2}=384.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

From compressed-liquid water properties, this corresponds to

\[
T_3 \approx 91.7^\circ\text{C}
\]

and

\[
s_3 \approx 1.214\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

For the entropy generation rate, the steady-state entropy balance for an adiabatic mixer is

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{gen}=6.0(1.214)-3.0(1.801)-3.0(0.531)=0.288\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_1 = 614.9 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 154.9 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.0 kg/s  
h_3 = 384.9 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 91.7 °C  
s_1 = 1.801 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.531 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.214 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.288 kW/K

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 160.0°C, 1.8 kg/s. Stream 2: 76.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Use steady, adiabatic mixing with negligible KE/PE changes.

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.8+4.9=6.7\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Properties:

- Stream 1 at \(0.2\ \text{MPa},\ 160^\circ\text{C}\): superheated steam  
  \[
  h_1 \approx 2789.0,\quad s_1 \approx 7.327
  \]
- Stream 2 at \(76^\circ\text{C}\): compressed liquid, approximated by saturated liquid at the same \(T\)  
  \[
  h_2 \approx 318.2,\quad s_2 \approx 1.026
  \]

Then
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}
=\frac{(1.8)(2789.0)+(4.9)(318.2)}{6.7}
\approx 982.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(0.2\ \text{MPa}\), saturation properties are approximately
\[
T_{\text{sat}}=120.2^\circ\text{C},\quad h_f=504.7,\quad h_{fg}=2201.6,\quad s_f=1.530,\quad s_{fg}=5.597
\]
Since \(h_f<h_3<h_g\), the exit is a saturated mixture, so
\[
T_3=T_{\text{sat}}=120.2^\circ\text{C}
\]
\[
x_3=\frac{h_3-h_f}{h_{fg}}\approx \frac{982.0-504.7}{2201.6}\approx 0.217
\]
\[
s_3=s_f+x_3 s_{fg}\approx 1.530+(0.217)(5.597)\approx 2.744\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy balance for adiabatic steady mixing:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}\approx (6.7)(2.744)-(1.8)(7.327)-(4.9)(1.026)\approx 0.166\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}=(298.15)(0.166)\approx 49.3\ \text{kW}
\]

For the second-law efficiency, use
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot X_{out}}{\dot X_{in}}=1-\frac{\dot X_{dest}}{\dot X_{in}}
\]
With dead-state liquid-water properties at \(25^\circ\text{C},0.1\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
h_0\approx 104.86\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_0\approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]
So the inlet flow exergies are
\[
\psi_1=(h_1-h_0)-T_0(s_1-s_0)\approx 609.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\psi_2=(h_2-h_0)-T_0(s_2-s_0)\approx 16.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\dot X_{in}=\dot m_1\psi_1+\dot m_2\psi_2\approx (1.8)(609.1)+(4.9)(16.9)\approx 1179\ \text{kW}
\]
\[
\eta_{II}\approx 1-\frac{49.3}{1179}\approx 0.958
\]

h_1 = 2789.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 318.2 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.7 kg/s  
h_3 = 982.0 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 120.2 °C  
s_1 = 7.327 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 1.026 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 2.744 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.166 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 49.3 kW  
η_II = 0.958

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.5 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 102.0°C, 2.4 kg/s. Stream 2: 58.0°C, 4.0 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Treat the water streams as compressed liquids at 0.5 MPa, with the usual approximation

- \(h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)\)
- \(s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)\)

and neglect KE/PE changes.

Mass balance:
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=2.4+4.0=6.4\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance for an adiabatic mixer:
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid water properties:

- at \(102^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1\approx 427.4\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1\approx 1.327\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- at \(58^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2\approx 242.8\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2\approx 0.804\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then
\[
h_3=\frac{\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2}{\dot m_3}
\approx 312.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
which corresponds to
\[
T_3 \approx 74.5^\circ\text{C}
\]
and
\[
s_3 \approx 1.008\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy generation rate:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\approx 0.0488\ \text{kW/K}
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}
=(298.15)(0.0488)\approx 14.5\ \text{kW}
\]

For the second-law efficiency of this adiabatic mixer, use
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot \Psi_{out}}{\dot \Psi_{in}}
\]
with liquid-water flow exergy approximated by
\[
\psi \approx c_p\big[(T-T_0)-T_0\ln(T/T_0)\big]+v(P-P_0)
\]
which gives \(\eta_{II}\approx 0.875\).

s_3 = 1.008 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 237.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. Stream 2: 33.0°C, 4.2 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady, adiabatic mixing-chamber balances:

1) Mass balance  
\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=4.9+4.2=9.1\ \text{kg/s}
\]

2) Property data from water tables  
- At \(P=1.2\) MPa, \(T_1=237^\circ\text{C}\) (superheated vapor):  
  \(h_1 \approx 2895\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 6.761\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- Stream 2 is a compressed liquid at \(33^\circ\text{C}\), so use the compressed-liquid approximation:  
  \(h_2 \approx h_f(33^\circ\text{C})+v(P-P_{sat}) \approx 139.4\ \text{kJ/kg}\)  
  \(s_2 \approx s_f(33^\circ\text{C}) \approx 0.479\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

3) Energy balance  
\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]
\[
h_3=\frac{(4.9)(2895)+(4.2)(139.4)}{9.1}\approx 1623.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(P=1.2\) MPa, this lies between saturated-liquid and saturated-vapor enthalpies, so the exit is a saturated mixture. Thus:
\[
T_3=T_{sat}(1.2\text{ MPa})\approx 188.0^\circ\text{C}
\]
Using saturated properties at 1.2 MPa gives:
\[
s_3 \approx 4.004\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

4) Entropy generation rate  
For steady adiabatic mixing:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]
\[
\dot S_{gen}=(9.1)(4.004)-(4.9)(6.761)-(4.2)(0.479)\approx 1.30\ \text{kW/K}
\]

5) Exergy destruction rate  
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}
\]
with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\):
\[
\dot X_{dest}\approx (298.15)(1.30)\approx 387\ \text{kW}
\]

6) Second-law efficiency  
Using the standard exergetic efficiency for a mixing chamber,
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3\psi_3}{\dot m_1\psi_1+\dot m_2\psi_2},
\qquad
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]
At the dead state \((25^\circ\text{C},0.1\text{ MPa})\), take
\[
h_0\approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_0\approx 0.3672\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
This gives approximately:
\[
\psi_1\approx 883.8,\quad \psi_2\approx 1.2,\quad \psi_3\approx 433.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
and
\[
\eta_{II}\approx \frac{(9.1)(433.9)}{(4.9)(883.8)+(4.2)(1.2)}\approx 0.911
\]

ṁ_3 = 9.1 kg/s

T_3 = 188.0 °C

s_3 = 4.004 kJ/(kg·K)

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using the compressed-liquid approximation for water at 0.7 MPa,
- \(h(T,p) \approx h_f(T)\) with a very small pressure correction,
- \(s(T,p) \approx s_f(T)\).

At 0.7 MPa, \(T_{sat}\approx 165^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the exit are compressed liquid.

Steady, adiabatic mixing:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2
\]

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Entropy balance for a steady adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

Exergy destruction:

\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0 \dot S_{gen}
\]

Second-law efficiency for the mixer:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot E_{x,out}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
=1-\frac{\dot X_{dest}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
\]

with flow exergy
\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]
at the dead state \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}\), \(P_0=0.1\text{ MPa}\).

Final results:

h_1 = 546.9 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 210.0 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 17.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 363.6 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 86.6 °C  
s_1 = 1.6348 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.1540 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.437 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 130.2 kW  
η_II = 0.761
response_text:
Using the compressed-liquid approximation for water at 0.7 MPa,
- \(h(T,p) \approx h_f(T)\) with a very small pressure correction,
- \(s(T,p) \approx s_f(T)\).

At 0.7 MPa, \(T_{sat}\approx 165^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the exit are compressed liquid.

Steady, adiabatic mixing:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2
\]

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Entropy balance for a steady adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

Exergy destruction:

\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0 \dot S_{gen}
\]

Second-law efficiency for the mixer:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot E_{x,out}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
=1-\frac{\dot X_{dest}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
\]

with flow exergy
\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]
at the dead state \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}\), \(P_0=0.1\text{ MPa}\).

Final results:

h_1 = 546.9 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 210.0 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 17.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 363.6 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 86.6 °C  
s_1 = 1.6348 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.1540 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.437 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 130.2 kW  
η_II = 0.761

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 389.0°C, 1.4 MPa with a velocity of 26.0 m/s and exits at 0.7 MPa. The nozzle isentropic efficiency is 91.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle isentropic efficiency is

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

Using superheated steam properties at the inlet state \(P_1=1.4\text{ MPa},\ T_1=389.0^\circ\text{C}\):

- find \(h_1,\ s_1\)
- then at \(P_2=0.7\text{ MPa}\) with \(s_{2s}=s_1\), find \(h_{2s}\)

From the tables/interpolation:

\[
h_1 \approx 3244.5\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 7.2670\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

At \(P_2=0.7\) MPa and \(s_{2s}=s_1\):

\[
h_{2s} \approx 3048.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now apply nozzle efficiency:

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2=3244.5-0.91(3244.5-3048.2)=3065.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then the exit velocity is

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2\times 1000\,(h_1-h_2)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{26.0^2+2(1000)(3244.5-3065.8)}
\approx 598.2\ \text{m/s}
\]

h_1 = 3244.5 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 7.2670 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 3048.2 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 598.2 m/s  
h_2 = 3065.8 kJ/kg

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 278.0°C, 4.1 MPa with a velocity of 40.0 m/s and exits at 0.56 MPa. The nozzle isentropic efficiency is 93.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using steam tables and the nozzle efficiency relation:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1=4.1\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=278.0^\circ\text{C}\) is **superheated steam**.  
   From superheated steam tables:

\[
h_1 \approx 2868\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 6.190\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

2. **Isentropic exit state**:  
   \(P_{2}=0.56\ \text{MPa}\), and for the isentropic case \(s_{2s}=s_1\).

   At \(0.56\ \text{MPa}\), use saturated properties and solve for quality:

\[
x_{2s}=\frac{s_1-s_f}{s_{fg}}
\]

This gives an isentropic exit enthalpy of approximately

\[
h_{2s}\approx 2499\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

3. **Nozzle isentropic efficiency**:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
\]

So,

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2 \approx 2525\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4. **Energy equation for adiabatic nozzle**:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)(1000)}
\]

with \(V_1=40.0\ \text{m/s}\), giving

\[
V_2 \approx 829\ \text{m/s}
\]

Final answers:

h_1 = 2868 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.190 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2499 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 829 m/s  
h_2 = 2525 kJ/kg

\]

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 293.0°C, 3.7 MPa with V₁ = 62.0 m/s and exits at 0.42 MPa. The nozzle efficiency is 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
For an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle efficiency is

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
\]

Using superheated steam tables at \(P_1=3.7\text{ MPa},\ T_1=293.0^\circ\text{C}\):

\[
h_1 \approx 2932 \text{ kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 6.363 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

At \(P_2=0.42\text{ MPa}\), the isentropic exit state satisfies \(s_{2s}=s_1\). From saturated-water data at \(0.42\text{ MPa}\) (approximately \(h_f=611.8,\ h_{fg}=2128.7,\ s_f=1.793,\ s_{fg}=5.087\)):

\[
x_{2s}=\frac{s_1-s_f}{s_{fg}}
      =\frac{6.363-1.793}{5.087}
      \approx 0.899
\]

\[
h_{2s}=h_f+x_{2s}h_{fg}
      \approx 611.8+(0.899)(2128.7)
      \approx 2524.6\text{ kJ/kg}
\]

Then

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2=2932-0.93(2932-2524.6)\approx 2553.1\text{ kJ/kg}
\]

Now apply the energy equation:

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{62.0^2+2(1000)(2932-2553.1)}\approx 8.72\times 10^2\text{ m/s}
\]

At \(P_2=0.42\text{ MPa}\), the actual exit state is also in the wet region:

\[
x_2=\frac{h_2-h_f}{h_{fg}}
    =\frac{2553.1-611.8}{2128.7}
    \approx 0.912
\]

\[
s_2=s_f+x_2 s_{fg}
   \approx 1.793+(0.912)(5.087)
   \approx 6.432\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

For an adiabatic one-in/one-out nozzle,

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

\[
s_{gen}\approx 6.432-6.363=0.069\text{ kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_1 = 2932.0 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.363 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2524.6 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 872.5 m/s  
h_2 = 2553.1 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 6.432 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.069 kJ/(kg·K)

V_2=\sqrt{74.0^2+2(1000)(3298.8-2836.0)}=964.9\ \text{m/s}
\]

\[
s_2 \approx 7.0260\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Since the nozzle is adiabatic,

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

\[
s_{gen}=7.0260-6.9850=0.0410\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_1 = 3298.8 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.9850 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Steam at 451.0°C / 4.1 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 70.0 m/s and exits at 0.62 MPa (η_nozzle = 92.0%). The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using superheated-steam properties and the adiabatic nozzle relations:

- Inlet state at \(P_1=4.1\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=451.0^\circ\text{C}\):
  \[
  h_1,\ s_1
  \]
- Isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.62\ \text{MPa}\) with \(s_{2s}=s_1\):
  \[
  h_{2s}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.92
  \]
  so
  \[
  h_2=h_1-0.92\,(h_1-h_{2s})
  \]
- Steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
  \]
- Entropy generation per unit mass:
  \[
  s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1
  \]
- Exergy destruction per unit mass:
  \[
  x_{\text{dest}}=T_0\,s_{\text{gen}},\qquad T_0=298.15\ \text{K}
  \]
- Using the exergy-based second-law efficiency for a nozzle:
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{\text{dest}}}
  =\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{\text{dest}}}
  \]

Final results:

h_1 = 3314.8 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.9014 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2826.4 kJ/kg

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 622.0 K and 0.51 MPa with a velocity of 50.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.18 MPa and η_nozzle = 95.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using constant-property ideal-gas relations for air:

1) Inlet enthalpy  
\[
h_1=c_pT_1=(1.005)(622.0)=625.11\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2) Isentropic exit temperature  
\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=622.0\left(\frac{0.18}{0.51}\right)^{0.4/1.4}
=461.93\ \text{K}
\]

Then
\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=(1.005)(461.93)=464.24\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

3) Nozzle efficiency  
\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]
so
\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
=625.11-0.95(625.11-464.24)
=472.28\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4) Energy equation for the adiabatic nozzle  
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
=\sqrt{50.0^2+2000(625.11-472.28)}
=555.1\ \text{m/s}
\]

For \(s_1\): with constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas analysis, absolute entropy depends on the chosen reference state. Taking the inlet as the entropy reference gives \(s_1=0\).

h_2 = 472.28 kJ/kg

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 561.0 K and 0.49 MPa with a velocity of 98.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.15 MPa and η_nozzle = 94.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using the ideal-gas, constant-\(c_p\) model:

\[
h=c_pT,\qquad R=c_p\left(1-\frac1k\right)=1.005\left(1-\frac1{1.4}\right)=0.2871\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

For the isentropic exit state of the nozzle,

\[
\frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}=\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
\]

\[
T_{2s}=561.0\left(\frac{0.15}{0.49}\right)^{(1.4-1)/1.4}=400.01\ \text{K}
\]

Then

\[
h_1=c_pT_1=1.005(561.0)=563.81\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=1.005(400.01)=402.01\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.94
\]

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
=563.81-0.94(563.81-402.01)
=411.72\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)\times 1000}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{(98.0)^2+2(563.81-411.72)\times 1000}
=560.16\ \text{m/s}
\]

Since only entropy differences matter here, I take the inlet state as the entropy reference, so \(s_1=0\).

h_2s = 402.01 kJ/kg

Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 846.0 K / 0.48 MPa with V₁ = 59.0 m/s. It exits at 0.2 MPa with η_nozzle = 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation.

Present your final answers in the following format:

Using ideal-gas, constant-\(c_p\) relations:

\[
h_1 = c_p T_1
\]

\[
T_{2s} = T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
\]

\[
h_{2s} = c_p T_{2s}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

Energy balance for the adiabatic nozzle:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass for an adiabatic nozzle:

\[
s_{\text{gen}} = s_2-s_1 = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\]

Numerics:

- \(h_2=850.23-0.93(850.23-662.07)=675.24\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(T_2=h_2/c_p=671.88\ \text{K}\)
- \(V_2=\sqrt{59.0^2+2000(850.23-675.24)}=594.5\ \text{m/s}\)
- \(s_{\text{gen}}=1.005\ln(671.88/846.0)-0.287\ln(0.2/0.48)=0.0196\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Since only entropy differences are determined from the given constant-\(c_p\) data, take the inlet as the entropy reference: \(s_1=0\).

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 622.0 K / 0.81 MPa with V₁ = 59.0 m/s. It exits at 0.12 MPa with η_nozzle = 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas relations and taking \(s_1\) as the entropy reference:

\[
h_1=c_pT_1
\]

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_{nozzle}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

so

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_{nozzle}(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

Then from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2(1000)}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2(1000)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass for an adiabatic one-in/one-out nozzle is

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

with

\[
s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right),
\qquad T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}
\]

- \(T_{2s}=622.0\left(\frac{0.12}{0.81}\right)^{0.4/1.4}=360.48\ \text{K}\)

- \(T_2=380.68/1.005=378.79\ \text{K}\)
- \(s_{gen}=1.005\ln(378.79/622.0)-0.287\ln(0.12/0.81)=0.0497\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

h_1=c_pT_1
\]

\[

\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_{nozzle}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

so

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_{nozzle}(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

Then from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2(1000)}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2(1000)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass for an adiabatic one-in/one-out nozzle is

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]

with

\[
s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right),
\qquad T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}
\]

Numerically:

- \(h_1=1.005(622.0)=625.11\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(T_{2s}=622.0\left(\frac{0.12}{0.81}\right)^{0.4/1.4}=360.48\ \text{K}\)
- \(h_{2s}=1.005(360.48)=362.28\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(h_2=625.11-0.93(625.11-362.28)=380.68\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(V_2=\sqrt{59.0^2+2(1000)(625.11-380.68)}=701.7\ \text{m/s}\)
- \(T_2=380.68/1.005=378.79\ \text{K}\)
- \(s_{gen}=1.005\ln(378.79/622.0)-0.287\ln(0.12/0.81)=0.0497\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Final answers:

h_1 = 625.11 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.0000 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 362.28 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 701.7 m/s  
h_2 = 380.68 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.0497 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.0497 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 621.0 K / 0.83 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 93.0 m/s and exits at 0.28 MPa (η_nozzle = 94.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using \(h=c_pT\) for air and referencing entropy to the dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):

\[
h_1=c_pT_1
\]
\[
s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_1}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_1}{P_0}\right)
\]

For the isentropic exit state:

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
\]
\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

Adiabatic nozzle energy equation:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
\]

Then

\[
s_2=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_0}\right),
\qquad T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}
\]

For an adiabatic nozzle:

\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
\]
\[
x_{dest}=T_0\,s_{gen}
\]

Second-law efficiency of the nozzle (exergy efficiency):

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{dest}}
\]
with
\[
\Delta ke=\frac{V_2^2-V_1^2}{2000}=h_1-h_2
\]

Final answers:

h_1 = 624.11 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.1300 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 457.94 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 566.61 m/s  
h_2 = 467.91 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.1524 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.0224 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 6.67 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.9590

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 675.0 K / 0.52 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 53.0 m/s and exits at 0.15 MPa (η_nozzle = 97.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using ideal-gas air with constant properties:

- \(h = c_p T\)
- \(s - s_0 = c_p \ln(T/T_0) - R \ln(P/P_0)\), with \(s_0=0\) at \((T_0,P_0)\)
- For the isentropic exit state:
  \[
  T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.97
  \]
- Steady adiabatic nozzle energy equation:
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
  \]
- Since the nozzle is adiabatic:
  \[
  s_{gen}=s_2-s_1
  \]
- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}
  \]
- Using the exergy (rational) second-law efficiency for a nozzle:
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke + x_{dest}}
  =\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)-T_0(s_1-s_2)}
  \]

Calculated values:

h_1 = 678.375 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.3480 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 475.564 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 629.5 m/s  
h_2 = 481.648 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.3606 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.01260 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 3.76 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.9813
